title: Ghost Traveler: Card Roams Without Legitimate Travel Footprint
narrative: Card appears in 4+ countries over 8-12 days with transactions totaling $3,200-$8,500, but zero charges to airlines (MCC 3000-3299), hotels (MCC 3501-3838), or car rentals (MCC 3351-3441). Instead, pattern shows CNP purchases at electronics retailers (MCC 5732, 5065) within 3-6 hours of each geographic shift, followed by point-of-sale transactions 18-36 hours later. Genuine travelers leave clear booking trails; this pattern suggests stolen card credentials being tested online, then physical card manufactured and used locally by mules in each region. Typical sequence: UK online purchase Â£840, then Romania ATM withdrawal 22 hours later, then Spain restaurant charges, then Thailand electronicsâ€”all without a single travel-related merchant. The card1_dist1 value exceeds 8000km between consecutive transactions, yet no corresponding travel merchant appears in 72-hour windows.
indicators: 4+ distinct countries (via addr1 or IP geolocation) within 8-12 day window
Zero transactions to MCC 3000-3299 (airlines), 3501-3838 (lodging), or 3351-3441 (car rental)
CNP transactions (ProductCD='W') within 3-6 hours of each geographic shift
card1_dist1 exceeds 8000km between consecutive transactions at least twice
Electronics/high-value retail (MCC 5732, 5065, 5944) appears in 75%+ of new-location transactions
Mean TransactionAmt per country: $650-$2,100 with card1_amt_zscore > 1.8
Time gap between online and physical card use in same country: 18-36 hours
summary: A card appears in four or more countries within 8-12 days with transactions totaling $3,200-$8,500, yet shows zero airline, hotel, or car rental charges—only electronics purchases and ATM withdrawals appearing within hours of each geographic jump, often with distances exceeding 8,000km between consecutive transactions.
reasoning: Legitimate international travelers leave clear booking trails; this pattern indicates stolen card credentials being tested online first, then physical cards manufactured and distributed to mules in each region who make high-value purchases locally, explaining the impossible travel speed and absence of any actual travel expenses.
indicator_explanations: Card appears in multiple countries within days without realistic travel pattern
No airline, hotel, or rental car purchases despite international movement
Online purchases happen immediately after each geographic location change
Card crosses continents between transactions with physically impossible timing
Nearly every new location targets electronics or high-ticket retail stores
Purchase amounts per country are elevated beyond normal cardholder spending
Physical card emerges in country shortly after online use there
